title: Hieracium placerophyllum
taxonomy: Hieracium placerophyllum is classified within the plant family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places Hieracium placerophyllum among the flowering plants, specifically within the tribe Lactuceae. Further classification places it within the subgenus Pilosella, a group of plants known for their small to medium-sized leaves and daisy-like flowers. Within the subgenus Pilosella, Hieracium placerophyllum is classified as a species of the Pilosella genus. The specific classification of Hieracium placerophyllum highlights its unique characteristics and relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Hieracium placerophyllum, also known as Mountain Hawkweed, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including wounds, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Mountain Hawkweed include flavonoids, such as luteolin and apigenin, as well as sesquiterpenoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Mountain Hawkweed is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is used to treat conditions such as eczema, acne, and digestive issues.
